member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.AgN6700
member.summary: N6700 Power Supplies

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.CLS
member.summary:
This command causes the following actions on the status system:
- Clears the Standard Event Status, Operation Status Event, and Questionable Status Event registers. - Clears the Status Byte and the Error Queue. - If *CLS immediately follows a program message terminator (<NL>), then the output queue and the MAV bit are also cleared.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.ESE
member.summary: This command programs the Standard Event Status Enable register bits. The programming determines which events of the Standard Event Status Event register (see *ESR?) are allowed to set the ESB (Event Summary Bit) of the Status Byte register.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.ESR
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Event register of the Standard Event Status group. The Event register is a read-only register, which stores (latches) all standard events. Reading the Standard Event Status Event register clears it.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.IDN
member.summary: This command reads the instrument's (mainframe) identification string which contains four comma-separated fields. The first field is the manufacturer's name, the second field is the instrument model number, the third field is the serial number, and the fourth field is the firmware revision.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.OPC
member.summary: The command is mainly used for program synchronization. It causes the instrument to set the OPC bit (bit 0) of the Standard Event Status register when the instrument has completed all pending operations sent before the *OPC command.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.OPT
member.summary: This query requests the mainframe to identify any installed options.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.RCL
member.summary: This command restores the instrument to a state that was previously stored in memory locations 0 through 1 with the *SAV command.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.RDT
member.summary: This query returns a description of all the power modules (channels) installed in a mainframe. Semicolons separate multiple channel descriptions.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.RST
member.summary: This command resets the volatile memory of the instrument to a factory-defined state.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.SAV
member.summary: This command stores the present state of the instrument to the specified location in non-volatile memory. Up to 2 states can be stored - in locations 0 and 1. Any state previously stored in the same location will be overwritten. Use the *RCL command to retrieve instrument states. Refer to Factory Reset State for a list of instrument settings that can be saved.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.SRE
member.summary: This command sets the value of the Service Request Enable register. This register determines which bits from the Status Byte register are summed to set the Master Status Summary (MSS) bit and the Request for Service (RQS) summary bit. A 1 in the bit position enables the corresponding event.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.STB
member.summary: This query reads the Status Byte register, which contains the status summary bits and the Output Queue MAV bit. The Status Byte register is a read-only register and the bits are not cleared when it is read.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.TRG
member.summary: This common command generates a trigger when the trigger subsystem has BUS selected as its source. The command has the same affect as the Group Execute Trigger (<GET>) command.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.TST
member.summary: This query causes the instrument to do a self-test and report any errors. A 0 indicates the instrument passed self-test. If all tests pass, you can have a high confidence that the instrument is operational.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SCPIClass.WAI
member.summary: This command instructs the instrument not to process any further commands until all pending operations are completed. Pending operations are as defined under the *OPC command.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.ABORtClass.ACQuire
member.summary: This command cancels any triggered measurements and returns the trigger system back to the Idle state. It also resets the WTG-meas bit in the Condition Status register.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.ABORtClass.TRANsient
member.summary: This command cancels any triggered actions and returns the trigger system back to the Idle state. ABORt:TRANsient also resets the WTG-tran bit in the Operation Condition Status register.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.ACQuire.ACQuireClass
member.summary: This command cancels any triggered measurements and returns the trigger system back to the Idle state. It also resets the WTG-meas bit in the Condition Status register.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.ACQuire.ACQuireClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: A Channel List expression

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.TRANsient.TRANsientClass
member.summary: This command cancels any triggered actions and returns the trigger system back to the Idle state. ABORt:TRANsient also resets the WTG-tran bit in the Operation Condition Status register.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ABORt.TRANsient.TRANsientClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: A Channel List expression

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.DATA
member.summary: This command enters a calibration value that you obtain by reading an external meter.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.DATE
member.summary: This command stores the date that the power module was last calibrated. The calibration date is stored in nonvolatile memory.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.DPRog
member.summary: This command initiates calibration of the current downprogrammer.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.LEVel
member.summary: This command is used to advance to the next level in the calibration.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.PASSword
member.summary: This command lets you change the calibration password. The new password is automatically stored in nonvolatile memory and does not have to be stored with CALibrate:SAVE.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.SAVE
member.summary: This command saves calibration constants in non-volatile memory after the calibration procedure has been completed.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CALibrate.CALibrateClass.STATe
member.summary: This command enables or disables calibration mode. Calibration mode must be enabled for the instrument to accept any calibration commands.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.INITiate.IMMediate.IMMediateClass.ACQuire
member.summary: This command enables the measurement trigger system. When a measurement trigger is initiated, an event on a selected trigger source causes the specified triggering action to occur. If the trigger system is not initiated, all triggers are ignored.

member.summary: This command enables the output trigger system. When an output trigger is initiated, an event on a selected trigger source causes the specified triggering action to occur. If the trigger system is not initiated, all triggers are ignored.

member.summary: This query initiates and triggers a measurement and returns an array containing the digitized output current in amperes. The sampling rate is set by SENSe:SWEep:TINTerval. The position of the trigger relative to the beginning of the data buffer is determined by SENSe:SWEep:OFFSet:POINTs. The number of points returned is set by SENSe:SWEep:POINts.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.MEASure.SCALar.CURRent.CURRentClass.DC
member.summary: This query initiates and triggers a measurement, and returns the DC output current in amperes. The default time interval and number of points settings yield a measurement time of 21 milliseconds per reading (1024 data points at 20.48 microsecond intervals). The default windowing function is Rectangular.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.OUTPutClass.STATe
member.summary: This command enables or disables the specified output channel(s). The enabled state is ON (1); the disabled state is OFF (0). The state of a disabled output is a condition of zero output voltage and zero source current. If output and sense relays are installed (Option 761), the relays will open when the output is disabled and close when the output is enabled.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.INHibit.INHibitClass.MODE
member.summary: This command selects the mode of operation of the Inhibit input (INH). The inhibit function shuts down ALL output channels in response to an external signal on the Inhibit input. If an output channel has been turned off by OUTPut:STATe, the inhibit function does not affect the output channel while it is in the OFF state. The Inhibit mode setting is stored in non-volatile memory.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.INHibit.MODE.MODEClass
member.summary: This command selects the mode of operation of the Inhibit input (INH). The inhibit function shuts down ALL output channels in response to an external signal on the Inhibit input. If an output channel has been turned off by OUTPut:STATe, the inhibit function does not affect the output channel while it is in the OFF state. The Inhibit mode setting is stored in non-volatile memory.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.INHibit.MODE.MODEClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: The mode of operation of the Inhibit input (INH). (Supported special values: "OFF" | "LATChing" | "LIVE")

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PON.PONClass.STATe
member.summary: This command determines if the power-on state is set to the *RST (RST) state or the instrument state stored in memory location 0 (RCL0). The parameter is saved in non-volatile memory. Instrument states can be stored using the *SAV command.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PON.STATe.STATeClass
member.summary: This command determines if the power-on state is set to the *RST (RST) state or the instrument state stored in memory location 0 (RCL0). The parameter is saved in non-volatile memory. Instrument states can be stored using the *SAV command.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PON.STATe.STATeClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: RST | RCL0

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PROTection.PROTectionClass.CLEar
member.summary: This command clears the latched protection status that disables the output when an over-voltage, over-temperature, over-current, inhibit signal, power-fail condition, or on some models, a power-limit condition is detected.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PROTection.PROTectionClass.COUPle
member.summary: This command enables/disables output coupling for protection faults. When enabled, ALL output channels are disabled when a protection fault occurs on any output channel. The enabled state is On (1); the disabled state is Off (0). When disabled, only the affected output channel is disabled when a protection fault is triggered.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PROTection.PROTectionClass.DELay
member.summary: This command sets the over-current protection delay. The over-current protection function will not be triggered on the specified output channel during the delay time. After the delay time has expired, the over-current protection function will be active. This prevents momentary changes in output status from triggering the over-current protection function.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.PROTection.CLEar.CLEarClass
member.summary: This command clears the latched protection status that disables the output when an over-voltage, over-temperature, over-current, inhibit signal, power-fail condition, or on some models, a power-limit condition is detected.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.STATeClass.PMODe
member.summary: This command sets the preferred mode for output on or output off transitions. It allows output state transitions to be optimized for either constant voltage or constant current operation. Turn-on and turn-off overshoots are minimized for the preferred mode of operation.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.COUPle.COUPleClass.CHANnel
member.summary: This command specifies which output channels in the mainframe are controlled by the output synchronization function. The output channels that have been synchronized or coupled will turn on and off together when any one of them is turned on or off, or when a signal is received from a digital connector pin that has been configured as an On couple or an Off couple pin.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.COUPle.COUPleClass.DOFFset
member.summary: This command specifies a maximum delay offset to synchronize the output state changes.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.COUPle.COUPleClass.STATe
member.summary: This command enables or disables output coupling or synchronization between multiple output channels. When output channels have been coupled and the Digital connector pins have been properly configured, enabling this function allows you to control the output state of multiple outputs in multiple mainframes by controlling the output state of one output channel.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.DELay.DELayClass.FALL
member.summary: This command sets the delay in seconds that the instrument waits before disabling the specified output. It affects on-to-off transitions including changes in the OUTPut:STATe as well as transitions due to changes in the voltage range or current range. It does NOT affect transitions to off caused by protection functions.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.DELay.DELayClass.RISE
member.summary: This command sets the delay in seconds that the instrument waits before enabling the specified output. It affects all off-to-on transitions including changes in the OUTPut:STATe as well as transitions due to OUTPut:PROTection:CLEar.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OUTPut.STATe.DELay.FALL.FALLClass

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SOURce.CURRent.PROTection.PROTectionClass.STATe
member.summary: This command enables or disables the over-current protection (OCP) function. The enabled state is On (1); the disabled state is Off (0). If the over-current protection function is enabled and the output goes into constant current operation, the output is disabled and the Questionable Condition status register OCP bit is set.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SOURce.POWer.POWerClass.LIMit
member.summary: This command sets the power limit on output channels. Units are in Watts. For the majority of Agilent N6700 and N6705A configurations, full power is available from all installed power modules. However, it is possible to configure a mainframe in which the combined ratings of the power modules exceed the power rating of the mainframe.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SOURce.VOLTage.SLEW.SLEWClass.IMMediate
member.summary: This command sets the voltage slew rate in volts per second. The slew rate setting affects all programmed voltage changes, including those due to the output state turning on or off. The slew rate can be set to any value between 0 and 9.9E+37. For very large values, the slew rate will be limited by the analog performance of the output circuit. The keywords MAX or INFinity set the slew rate to maximum.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SOURce.VOLTage.SLEW.IMMediate.IMMediateClass
member.summary: This command sets the voltage slew rate in volts per second. The slew rate setting affects all programmed voltage changes, including those due to the output state turning on or off. The slew rate can be set to any value between 0 and 9.9E+37. For very large values, the slew rate will be limited by the analog performance of the output circuit. The keywords MAX or INFinity set the slew rate to maximum.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.STATusClass.PRESet
member.summary: This command sets all defined bits in the status system's PTR registers and clears all bits in the NTR and Enable registers.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.OPERationClass.CONDition
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Operation Status Condition register. The Condition register is a read-only register, which holds the live (unlatched) operational status of the instrument. Reading the Operation Condition register does not clear it.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.OPERationClass.ENABle
member.summary: This command and its query set and read the value of the Operation Status Enable register. The Enable register is a mask for enabling specific bits from the Operation Event register to set the operation summary bit (OPER) of the Status Byte register. This bit (bit 7) is the logical OR of all the Operational Event register bits that are enabled by the Operation Status Enable register.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.OPERationClass.EVENt
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Operation Status Event register. The Event register is a read-only register, which stores (latches) all events that are passed by the Operation NTR and/or PTR filter. Reading the Operation Status Event register clears it.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.OPERationClass.NTRansition
member.summary: These command sets and reads the value of the Operation NTR (Negative-Transition) register. This register serves as a polarity filter between the Operation Condition and Operation Event registers.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.OPERationClass.PTRansition
member.summary: These command sets and reads the value of the Operation PTR (Positive-Transition) register. This register serves as a polarity filter between the Operation Condition and Operation Event registers.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.CONDition.CONDitionClass
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Operation Status Condition register. The Condition register is a read-only register, which holds the live (unlatched) operational status of the instrument. Reading the Operation Condition register does not clear it.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.CONDition.CONDitionClass.Query(System.String,System.Int32[]@)
member.param: One or more channels. (@2) - channel 2 , (@1,4) - channels 1 and 4, (@1:3) - channels 1 through 3.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.ENABle.ENABleClass
member.summary: This command and its query set and read the value of the Operation Status Enable register. The Enable register is a mask for enabling specific bits from the Operation Event register to set the operation summary bit (OPER) of the Status Byte register. This bit (bit 7) is the logical OR of all the Operational Event register bits that are enabled by the Operation Status Enable register.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.OPERation.ENABle.ENABleClass.Command(System.Int32,System.String)

member.summary: This query returns the value of the Operation Status Event register. The Event register is a read-only register, which stores (latches) all events that are passed by the Operation NTR and/or PTR filter. Reading the Operation Status Event register clears it.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.PRESet.PRESetClass
member.summary: This command sets all defined bits in the status system's PTR registers and clears all bits in the NTR and Enable registers.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.QUEStionable.QUEStionableClass.CONDition
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Questionable Status Condition register. The Condition register is a read-only register, which holds the live (unlatched) questionable status of the instrument. Reading the Questionable Condition register does not clear it.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.QUEStionable.QUEStionableClass.ENABle
member.summary: This command and its query set and read the value of the Questionable Status Enable register. The Enable register is a mask for enabling specific bits from the Questionable Event register to set the questionable summary bit (QUES) of the Status Byte register. This bit (bit 3) is the logical OR of all the Questionable Event register bits that are enabled by the Questionable Status Enable register.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.QUEStionable.QUEStionableClass.EVENt
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Questionable Status Event register. The Event register is a read-only register, which stores (latches) all events that are passed by the Questionable NTR and/or PTR filter. Reading the Questionable Status Event register clears it.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STATus.QUEStionable.QUEStionableClass.NTRansition
member.summary: These command sets and reads the value of the Questionable NTR (Negative-Transition) register. This register serves as a polarity filter between the Questionable Condition and Questionable Event registers.

member.summary: These command sets and reads the value of the Questionable PTR (Positive-Transition) register. This register serves as a polarity filter between the Questionable Condition and Questionable Event registers.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SYSTem.COMMunicate.TCPip.TCPipClass.CONTrol
member.summary: This query returns the control connection port number. This is used to open a control socket connection to the instrument. Unlike the data socket, which uses a fixed port number (5025 for Agilent instruments), the port number for a control socket varies, and must be obtained using this query.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SYSTem.COMMunicate.TCPip.CONTrol.CONTrolClass

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SYSTem.GROup.GROupClass.CATalog
member.summary: This query returns information about channels that are grouped. The defined groups are enclosed in quotes.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SYSTem.GROup.GROupClass.DEFine
member.summary: This command defines a list of output channels as a paralleled group. This effectively creates a single output with higher current and power capability. You can group up to four channels per mainframe. All channels in a group must have identical model numbers and have identical options installed.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SYSTem.GROup.GROupClass.DELete
member.summary: This command removes the indicated channel from a group. It leaves the other channels in the group intact.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.CLS.CLSClass
member.summary:
This command causes the following actions on the status system:
- Clears the Standard Event Status, Operation Status Event, and Questionable Status Event registers. - Clears the Status Byte and the Error Queue. - If *CLS immediately follows a program message terminator (<NL>), then the output queue and the MAV bit are also cleared.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ESE.ESEClass
member.summary: This command programs the Standard Event Status Enable register bits. The programming determines which events of the Standard Event Status Event register (see *ESR?) are allowed to set the ESB (Event Summary Bit) of the Status Byte register.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ESE.ESEClass.Command(System.Int32)
member.param: A decimal value which corresponds to the binary-weighted sum of the bits in the register. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ESE.ESEClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: A decimal value which corresponds to the binary-weighted sum of the bits in the register. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.ESR.ESRClass
member.summary: This query returns the value of the Event register of the Standard Event Status group. The Event register is a read-only register, which stores (latches) all standard events. Reading the Standard Event Status Event register clears it.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.IDN.IDNClass
member.summary: This command reads the instrument's (mainframe) identification string which contains four comma-separated fields. The first field is the manufacturer's name, the second field is the instrument model number, the third field is the serial number, and the fourth field is the firmware revision.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OPC.OPCClass
member.summary: The command is mainly used for program synchronization. It causes the instrument to set the OPC bit (bit 0) of the Standard Event Status register when the instrument has completed all pending operations sent before the *OPC command.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.OPT.OPTClass
member.summary: This query requests the mainframe to identify any installed options.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.RCL.RCLClass
member.summary: This command restores the instrument to a state that was previously stored in memory locations 0 through 1 with the *SAV command.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.RCL.RCLClass.Command(System.Int32)
member.param: The identity of the memory register to recall. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.RCL.RCLClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: The identity of the memory register to recall. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.RDT.RDTClass
member.summary: This query returns a description of all the power modules (channels) installed in a mainframe. Semicolons separate multiple channel descriptions.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.RST.RSTClass
member.summary: This command resets the volatile memory of the instrument to a factory-defined state.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SAV.SAVClass
member.summary: This command stores the present state of the instrument to the specified location in non-volatile memory. Up to 2 states can be stored - in locations 0 and 1. Any state previously stored in the same location will be overwritten. Use the *RCL command to retrieve instrument states. Refer to Factory Reset State for a list of instrument settings that can be saved.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SAV.SAVClass.Command(System.Int32)
member.param: The identity of the memory register in which the current instrument state should be stored. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SAV.SAVClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: The identity of the memory register in which the current instrument state should be stored. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SRE.SREClass
member.summary: This command sets the value of the Service Request Enable register. This register determines which bits from the Status Byte register are summed to set the Master Status Summary (MSS) bit and the Request for Service (RQS) summary bit. A 1 in the bit position enables the corresponding event.

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SRE.SREClass.Command(System.Int32)
member.param: A decimal value which corresponds to the binary-weighted sum of the bits in the register. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: M:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.SRE.SREClass.Command(System.String)
member.param: A decimal value which corresponds to the binary-weighted sum of the bits in the register. (Supported special values: "MINimum" | "MAXimum")

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.STB.STBClass
member.summary: This query reads the Status Byte register, which contains the status summary bits and the Output Queue MAV bit. The Status Byte register is a read-only register and the bits are not cleared when it is read.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.TRG.TRGClass
member.summary: This common command generates a trigger when the trigger subsystem has BUS selected as its source. The command has the same affect as the Group Execute Trigger (<GET>) command.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.TST.TSTClass
member.summary: This query causes the instrument to do a self-test and report any errors. A 0 indicates the instrument passed self-test. If all tests pass, you can have a high confidence that the instrument is operational.

member.name: T:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.SCPI.WAI.WAIClass
member.summary: This command instructs the instrument not to process any further commands until all pending operations are completed. Pending operations are as defined under the *OPC command.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.DefaultTimeout
member.summary:
DefaultTimeout specifies the timeout, in milliseconds, for queries issued to this instrument.
A timeout will occur if there is no response from the instrument within the specified duration.
A value of -1 is used to specify no timeout.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.ErrorQueryBehavior
member.summary:
ErrorQueryBehavior specifies whether Command Expert automatically retrieves errors from the instrument's SCPI error queue by sending SYST:ERR? queries. Command Expert displays errors retrieved from the SCPI error queue in the Results column of the Sequence pane.
The supported values for ErrorQueryBehavior are:
None - SYST:ERR? queries will not be automatically sent.
ErrorQueryPerCommand - a :SYST:ERR? query will be sent after each command, and after each query that times out.
ErrorQueryPerCommandAndQuery - a :SYST:ERR? query will be sent after each command and after each query.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.OpcBehavior
member.summary:
OpcBehavior specifies whether Command Expert automatically sends a *OPC? query after each command.
The supported values for OpcBehavior are:
None - *OPC? queries will not be automatically sent to the instrument.
OpcPerCommand - an *OPC? query will be sent after every command, and no further commands or queries will be sent until the *OPC? returns.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.OmitDefaultKeywords
member.summary: OmitDefaultKeywords specifies whether to omit default keywords when sending SCPI commands.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.UseShortFormKeywords
member.summary: UseShortFormKeywords specifies whether to use the short form of SPCI keywords when sending SCPI commands.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.IgnoredErrors
member.summary:
IgnoredErrors is a string that specifies the list of SCPI error codes that will be ignored during execution.
For example, setting IgnoredErrors to the string "1:100,301:400" specifies that error codes from 1 to 100 inclusive and 301 to 400 inclusive will be ignored.
When setting IgnoredErrors, the input string should contain a list of zero or more items, separated by commas. Each item is either an integer, or a range of the form X:Y where X and Y are integers. The integers can be positive, 0, or negative.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.AddToIgnoredErrors
member.summary:
AddToIgnoredErrors adds the specified error codes to IgnoredErrors (the list of SCPI error codes ignored during execution).
For example, invoking AddToIgnoredErrors with the string "1:100,301:400" will add the error codes from 1 to 100 inclusive and 301 to 400 inclusive.
When invoking AddToIgnoredErrors, the input string should contain a list of zero or more items, separated by commas. Each item is either an integer, or a range of the form X:Y where X and Y are integers. The integers can be positive, 0, or negative.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.RemoveFromIgnoredErrors
member.summary:
RemoveFromIgnoredErrors removes the specified error codes from IgnoredErrors (the list of SCPI error codes ignored during execution).
For example, invoking RemoveFromIgnoredErrors with the string "1:100,301:400" will remove the error codes from 1 to 100 inclusive and 301 to 400 inclusive.
When invoking RemoveFromIgnoredErrors, the input string should contain a list of zero or more items, separated by commas. Each item is either an integer, or a range of the form X:Y where X and Y are integers. The integers can be positive, 0, or negative.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.Command
member.summary: Sends a SCPI command that does not return a response from the instrument. Use the transport resource Query if the instrument returns a response.

member.name: P:Agilent.CommandExpert.ScpiNet.AgN6700_C_02_18.Transport.TransportClass.Query
member.summary: Sends a SCPI query string directly to the instrument and returns the result.

member.summary: CommandBatching specifies SCPI command batching behavior.

The supported values for OpcBehavior are:
